questions.text: The combination of a pentose sugar with a base result in a compound known as:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A nucleotide is a fundamental building block of nucleic acids, which include DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid) and RNA (ribonucleic acid). Nucleotides play a central role in storing, transmitting, and processing genetic information within cells. Nucleotides are the fundamental units that make up the genetic code and play a central role in the storage, transmission, and processing of genetic information. They are essential for life and the functioning of living organisms.

A nucleoside is a fundamental component of nucleic acids (DNA and RNA) and serves as the structural building block of these important molecules. A nucleoside is formed by the combination of two components: a nitrogenous base and a pentose sugar. Unlike nucleotides, nucleosides do not contain a phosphate group. Nucleosides are essential components in the structure and function of nucleic acids. They serve as the foundation from which nucleotides are built and, ultimately, the genetic information of an organism is stored, transmitted, and processed.

Nucleic acids are one of the two major classes of macromolecules (the other being proteins) and are essential to the storage and transmission of genetic information in living organisms. They come in two primary forms: deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) and ribonucleic acid (RNA).

A polynucleotide is a biomolecule composed of a linear chain of nucleotides. Nucleotides are the building blocks of nucleic acids, which include DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid) and RNA (ribonucleic acid). Polynucleotides are essential for the storage, transmission, and expression of genetic information in living organisms.

questions.explanation: The compound formed by a combination of a base and a pentose sugar is called nucleoside,A nucleoside and a phosphoric acid combine to form a nucleotide. Nucleotides join to form polynucleotides. Nucleic acid is made up of polynucleotide.

questions.explanation: Adenine, guanine, and cytosine are the N2 containing bases present in both DNA and RNA. Uracil is present in RNA and absent in DNA while thymine is present in DNA, and absent in RNA.

options.explanation: Glyceraldehyde is a simple sugar and a three-carbon carbohydrate. It is an aldotriose, which means it has an aldehyde functional group. Glyceraldehyde is a significant molecule in biochemistry, particularly in the context of glycolysis, a fundamental metabolic pathway.

Dihydroxyacetone (DHA) is a simple carbohydrate and a three-carbon sugar with two hydroxyl (OH) groups, giving it its name. It is not commonly used as a source of energy in living organisms, but rather it has important applications in various industries and especially in the field of cosmetics and self-tanning products.

Glucose is a simple sugar and a crucial source of energy for living organisms. It is a type of carbohydrate and is one of the primary molecules used by the body to produce ATP (adenosine triphosphate), which is the primary energy currency of cells. Glucose is commonly found in various foods, including carbohydrates such as sugars and starches, and it is also produced by the body through the breakdown of more complex carbohydrates during digestion.

Ribose is a type of sugar and a component of RNA (ribonucleic acid), which is one of the two major types of nucleic acids found in cells. The other major nucleic acid is DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid). Ribose is a pentose sugar, meaning it has five carbon atoms in its molecular structure.

questions.text: Which molecular structure of enzyme is essential for activity of enzyme?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A linear polypeptide with a specific sequence and number of amino acids is called primary structure. It is shown by all proteins at the time of their synthesis on ribosomal surface. After synthesis a protein does not remain in its primary structure but can be changed into some other structural conformations (particular form, shape or structure).

A helical (a-helix) or flattened sheets (13-pleated sheet) like structures which are established by H-bonding between opposite charge bearing groups of different amino acids are called secondary structures.
Some proteins exist in very complex structure in which more than one globule is attached together by hydrophobic interaction. Such structures are called quaternary structures.
In some proteins the linear polypeptide is changed into a-helix, then ahelix fold again and again by ionic bonds and disulfide bridges to form a globular shaped structure, the tertiary structure.

questions.explanation: Globular structure of enzyme has a very important role in its activity and this globular structure attains at least tertiary level structure.

questions.text: The compounds which on hydrolysis yield polyhydroxy aldehyde or ketone subunits are:

This option is incorrect. Proteins are polymers of amino acids hence, on hydrolysis they yield amino acids.

This option is incorrect. Polynucleotides are polymers made up of nucleotide monomers, which are the building blocks of DNA and RNA. Hydrolysis of polynucleotides involves the breaking of the phosphodiester bonds that link the nucleotides together. The products of hydrolysis of polynucleotides are nucleotides.

This option is correct. Hydrolysis of polysaccharides gives polyhydroxy aldehyde or ketone subunits. Polysaccharides are complex carbohydrates that are made up of repeating units of monosaccharides, which are simple sugars. During hydrolysis of polysaccharides, water is added to break the glycosidic bonds that link the monosaccharide units together. This results in the separation of the monosaccharide units from each other. The monosaccharide units that are produced are polyhydroxy aldehyde or ketone subunits.

questions.explanation: Carbohydrates are polyhydroxy aldehydes or ketones or complex substances which on hydrolysis yield polyhydroxy aldehyde or ketone subunits. Proteins are polymers of amino acids, Lipids are heterogeneous groups of compounds related to fatty acids, while the polynucleotide chain is made up of nucleotides.

questions.text: Which of the following type of carbohydrate has high molecular weight and is sparingly soluble in water?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Monosaccharides are the simplest form of carbohydrates, also known as simple sugars. They are the basic building blocks of more complex carbohydrates and play a crucial role in providing energy for cellular processes. They have low molecular weight and are soluble in water because they only contain 1 saccharide unit.
Disaccharides are carbohydrates composed of two monosaccharide units joined together by a glycosidic bond.

Oligosaccharides are carbohydrates composed of a small number of monosaccharide units (typically 3 to 10) linked together by glycosidic bonds. They are more complex than disaccharides but simpler than polysaccharides.

Polysaccharides are large, complex carbohydrates composed of many monosaccharide units (often hundreds or thousands) linked together by glycosidic bonds. They serve primarily as energy storage molecules or structural components in cells and organisms. Therefore, they are less soluble in water.

questions.explanation: Polysaccharides are made up of many monosaccharides subunits and have higher molecular weight as compared to monosaccharides and oligosaccharides. Polysaccharides are sparingly soluble in water while monosaccharides has high solubility.

questions.text: Which of the following holds the alpha helix of protein in its place?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: In an alpha-helix, hydrogen bonds play a fundamental role in stabilizing the secondary structure. The alpha-helix is a coiled and compact arrangement of the protein backbone, and hydrogen bonds form between the carbonyl oxygen (C=O) of one amino acid and the amide hydrogen (N-H) of another amino acid, which is four residues ahead in the sequence. This pattern repeats along the entire length of the helix, creating a stable and regular structure.

A peptide bond forms between the carboxyl group (C=O) of one amino acid and the amino group (N-H) of another amino acid during protein synthesis, creating a covalent link between consecutive amino acids in the polypeptide chain. Peptide bonds do contribute to the overall polypeptide backbone but are not directly involved in the formation of the alpha helix's hydrogen-bonded structure.

The R-group, also known as the side chain, is the variable part of the amino acid that determines its specific properties. It extends outward from the alpha carbon of the amino acid backbone. While the R-groups of different amino acids can influence the local folding patterns (secondary structures) of proteins, they do not participate in the formation of the alpha helix's specific hydrogen-bonded structure.

Disulfide bridges are covalent bonds formed between two cysteine residues in a protein. They are responsible for stabilizing the tertiary and quaternary structures of proteins by forming strong, permanent links between specific amino acid side chains. While disulfide bonds are important for protein stability, they are not involved in the formation of alpha helices.

questions.explanation: The a-helix is a very uniform geometric structure with 3.6 amino acids in each turn of the helix. The helical structure is kept by the formation of hydrogen bonds among the amino acids molecules in successive turns of the spiral.

questions.text: The number and sequence of amino acids along a polypeptide chain is called _ structure of a protein.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The secondary structure of a protein refers to the local folding patterns that result from hydrogen bonding between nearby amino acid residues. The two most common types of secondary structures are the alpha helix and beta sheet. The primary sequence influences the formation of these structures because certain amino acid residues have a greater propensity to participate in these hydrogen bonding interactions, based on their side chain properties and positions in the sequence.

Some proteins consist of multiple polypeptide chains (subunits) that come together to form a functional protein complex. The quaternary structure is the arrangement of these subunits and the interactions between them. The amino acid sequence of each subunit is crucial because it determines how the individual chains come together to form the functional protein complex.

The primary structure of a protein refers to the linear sequence of amino acids that are covalently linked together by peptide bonds. The specific order of amino acids in the polypeptide chain is crucial because it determines the protein's overall chemical properties, interactions, and function. Even a slight change in the amino acid sequence can significantly alter the protein's structure and function.

The tertiary structure of a protein is the three-dimensional arrangement of the entire polypeptide chain. It is stabilized by various interactions such as hydrogen bonds, hydrophobic interactions, ionic bonds, and disulfide bridges between different amino acid residues in the primary sequence. The specific arrangement of amino acids in the primary structure dictates the folding and overall shape of the protein in its functional form.

questions.explanation: The primary structure of a protein is described by number, sequence and type of amino acids in a polypeptide chains.

questions.text: Which of the following is an unsaturated “fatty acid”?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is a saturated fatty acid. Butyric acid is a short-chain fatty acid with four carbon atoms and is typically found in small quantities in certain dairy products and butter. It is a saturated fatty acid because it contains only single covalent bonds between its carbon atoms.

This is a saturated fatty acid. Stearic acid is an 18-carbon saturated fatty acid, meaning it contains no double bonds between its carbon atoms. It is commonly found in animal fats, cocoa butter, and certain vegetable fats.

Oleic acid is an 18-carbon monounsaturated fatty acid with one double bond (a single point of unsaturation) between carbon atoms. It is one of the most common unsaturated fatty acids found in various vegetable oils, such as olive oil, as well as in animal fats.

This is a saturated fatty acid. Palmitic acid is a 16-carbon saturated fatty acid, containing no double bonds between its carbon atoms. It is one of the most abundant saturated fatty acids in nature and is found in various animal and plant fats.

questions.explanation: Butyric acid, palmitic acid and stearic acids examples of saturated fatty acids. Oleic acid is an example of mono-unsaturated fatty acid and has a double bond between C9 and C10.

questions.text: Starch is present in tuber, fruits and grains but absent in animal cells. Instead animals have a substance stored in liver and muscles known as:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Glucose is not stored. It changes into glycogen to be stored.

Glycogen is a highly branched polysaccharide and serves as a form of energy storage in animals, including humans. It is commonly found in the liver and muscle cells, where it functions as a readily available source of glucose when the body needs energy.

Galactose is a monosaccharide, a type of simple sugar, and is one of the essential dietary carbohydrates. It is chemically classified as a hexose because it contains six carbon atoms in its structure. Galactose is commonly found in various foods and is a component of lactose, the primary sugar present in milk.

questions.text: The sum of all chemical reactions taking place within a cell is called:

options.explanation: Thermoregulation is the process by which organisms maintain their body temperature within a relatively narrow and optimal range despite changes in the external environment. It is a critical physiological mechanism that helps to ensure the stability of cellular and metabolic processes necessary for the proper functioning of living organisms.

Osmoregulation is the physiological process by which organisms control the concentration of water and solutes within their bodies to maintain internal osmotic balance and prevent excessive water loss or gain. It is a critical function for the survival and proper functioning of living organisms, as maintaining the right balance of water and solutes is essential for cellular processes and overall health.

Metabolism is the set of biochemical processes that occur within an organism to maintain life. It involves the conversion of food (nutrients) into energy, as well as the synthesis and breakdown of biomolecules necessary for cellular function, growth, and reproduction.

Isomerism is a phenomenon in chemistry where two or more chemical compounds have the same molecular formula but different chemical structures or arrangements of atoms. These compounds are known as isomers, and they exhibit distinct physical and chemical properties despite sharing the same number of atoms of each element.

Digestion is the process by which food is broken down and converted into smaller, simpler molecules that can be absorbed and utilized by the body. It is a crucial part of the digestive system, responsible for extracting nutrients and energy from the food we eat, and supporting various physiological functions.

Photosynthesis is a vital biological process that occurs in green plants, algae, and some bacteria. It is the process by which these organisms convert light energy from the sun into chemical energy in the form of glucose (a type of carbohydrate) while releasing oxygen as a byproduct. Photosynthesis is a key process in the ecosystem, as it is the primary source of energy for most living organisms on Earth.
Respiration and digestion are not anabolic processes.

questions.text: Most abundant organic compounds of a cell are:

options.explanation: Water is not an organic compound.

Proteins make up around 10-18% of the dry weight of mammalian cells. Proteins are essential for various cellular functions, including enzyme catalysis, structural support, cell signaling, and transport of molecules.

Carbohydrates make up a smaller percentage of the dry weight, typically around 1-2% in mammalian cells. Carbohydrates play roles in energy storage (e.g., glycogen in liver and muscle cells) and as structural components (e.g., glycoproteins and glycolipids in cell membranes).

Lipids generally account for approximately 1-3% of the dry weight of mammalian cells. While lipids are essential components of cell membranes and play important roles in energy storage and signaling, their overall content is lower than that of proteins.

questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Water acts as a thermo-stabilizer due to its high heat capacity. Heat capacity refers to the amount of heat energy required to raise the temperature of a substance. Water has a relatively high heat capacity, which means it can absorb and store a significant amount of heat energy without a substantial increase in temperature. This property of water allows it to stabilize temperature changes in its surroundings. It helps regulate the Earth's climate by absorbing heat during the day and releasing it at night, preventing rapid temperature fluctuations. Additionally, water's high heat capacity plays a crucial role in maintaining stable temperatures within living organisms, protecting them from extreme temperature variations.

Water is often called the "universal solvent" due to its ability to dissolve a wide variety of substances. This property arises from water's polarity and its ability to form hydrogen bonds. Water molecules have a partially positive hydrogen end and a partially negative oxygen end, which makes them attracted to other polar or charged molecules. When a solute is added to water, the water molecules surround and separate the solute particles, allowing them to disperse evenly throughout the water. This ability to dissolve and transport various substances makes water a vital solvent for biological processes, such as nutrient transport in cells and the dissolution of biomolecules like proteins and carbohydrates.

Water is not considered an inert medium. An inert medium refers to a substance that does not react chemically with other substances. Water, on the other hand, can participate in chemical reactions due to its polar nature and ability to form hydrogen bonds. It can act as a reactant or a product in various chemical reactions, including acid-base reactions, hydrolysis reactions, and oxidation-reduction reactions.

While water can participate in chemical reactions, it is not primarily known for being a reactive medium. Water is relatively stable and does not readily undergo spontaneous reactions. However, under certain conditions, such as in the presence of catalysts or specific reactants, water can participate in chemical reactions, such as hydrolysis or dehydration reactions. These reactions typically involve breaking and forming covalent bonds within water molecules and other compounds.

questions.text: The water molecules remain attached together and do not separate because of this bonding:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Non-covalent bonds are a class of chemical interactions that do not involve the sharing of electrons between atoms. Instead, they are relatively weaker interactions, including hydrogen bonds, van der Waals forces, and electrostatic interactions. While hydrogen bonds are a type of non-covalent bond, it is more specific to mention "hydrogen bonds" rather than the broader term "non-covalent."

Ionic bonds involve the complete transfer of electrons from one atom to another, resulting in the formation of positively charged ions (cations) and negatively charged ions (anions). These oppositely charged ions are held together by electrostatic attractions, forming a strong bond. Water does not form ionic bonds with itself, so this option is not the correct one.

The correct option is hydrogen bonds. Water molecules are held together by hydrogen bonds. A hydrogen bond is a type of non-covalent bond formed between a partially positive hydrogen atom and a partially negative atom of another molecule or within the same molecule. In water, each water molecule contains two hydrogen atoms covalently bonded to a central oxygen atom. The oxygen atom is more electronegative than hydrogen, resulting in a partial negative charge on the oxygen atom and partial positive charges on the hydrogen atoms. These partial charges allow water molecules to form hydrogen bonds with each other. The hydrogen of one water molecule is attracted to the oxygen of another water molecule, creating a network of hydrogen bonds that hold the water molecules together in a cohesive structure.

Hydrophobic interactions are interactions between nonpolar molecules that exclude water. Water molecules tend to cluster together to minimize their contact with nonpolar substances. While hydrophobic interactions play a role in the self-assembly of nonpolar molecules, they do not directly account for the cohesion of water molecules.

questions.text: Which property of water plays an important role in regulation of heat produced by oxidation?

options.explanation: Heat capacity refers to the amount of heat energy required to raise the temperature of a substance. Water does have a relatively high heat capacity, which allows it to absorb and store a significant amount of heat energy without a substantial increase in temperature. This property contributes to water's role as a thermo-stabilizer,

The heat of vaporization is the amount of heat energy required to convert a liquid into a gas at a constant temperature and pressure. Water has a particularly high heat of vaporization compared to many other liquids. When water absorbs heat and undergoes vaporization, it takes a considerable amount of heat from its surroundings to break the hydrogen bonds and transition from a liquid to a gaseous state. This process is highly endothermic and results in a cooling effect on the surrounding environment. In the context of oxidation, heat of vaporization plays a crucial role in the regulation of heat produced by exothermic oxidation reactions. Exothermic reactions release heat energy, and if left unchecked, they can lead to an increase in temperature in the surrounding environment. However, in the presence of water, as the temperature rises due to oxidation, water absorbs this excess heat and undergoes vaporization. This endothermic process helps to moderate the temperature rise, preventing the system from overheating.

Water's dipole nature, resulting from its polar covalent bonds and hydrogen bonding, is responsible for many of its unique properties, including its ability to dissolve polar and charged substances, its high surface tension, and its cohesive properties. However, the dipole nature of water is not directly related to the regulation of heat produced by oxidation.

Water can undergo a process of ionization, where a small fraction of water molecules dissociate into positively charged hydrogen ions (H+) and negatively charged hydroxide ions (OH-). This process is responsible for water's ability to act as a weak electrolyte. However, the ionization of water does not play a significant role in the regulation of heat produced by oxidation.

options.explanation: Specific heat capacity refers to the amount of heat energy required to raise the temperature of a substance by a specific amount. While water does have a high specific heat capacity, this property is not directly related to maintaining the integrity of lipid membranes.

Cohesion refers to the attraction between water molecules, causing them to stick together, while adhesion refers to the attraction between water molecules and other substances. Both cohesion and adhesion are important properties of water, contributing to phenomenon like surface tension and capillary action. However, they are not directly involved in maintaining the integrity of lipid membranes.

Hydrogen bonding is a type of non-covalent bonding between water molecules, which gives water many of its unique properties, such as high heat of vaporization, high surface tension, and solvent capabilities. While hydrogen bonding is crucial for various functions in living organisms, it is not the primary property that helps maintain the integrity of lipid membranes.

In biological systems, such as cell membranes, lipids play a fundamental role in forming a barrier that separates the aqueous environments inside and outside the cell. The hydrophobic exclusion property of lipids helps maintain the integrity of lipid membranes by preventing water from permeating through the hydrophobic core of the membrane. This exclusion of water ensures that the lipid bilayer remains intact and impermeable to water-soluble substances, while allowing the passage of hydrophobic molecules and small nonpolar molecules necessary for cell function.

questions.text: Water acts as a temperature stabilizer for many organisms in the environment because of its:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Surface tension refers to the property of the surface of a liquid that allows it to resist external forces, effectively minimizing its surface area. Water does have a relatively high surface tension due to the cohesive forces between its molecules. While high surface tension has various biological implications, such as the ability of certain insects to walk on water, it is not the primary reason why water acts as a temperature stabilizer.

The latent heat of vaporization is the amount of heat energy required to convert a liquid into a gas at a constant temperature and pressure. Water has a particularly high latent heat of vaporization compared to many other liquids. While this property is essential for processes like evaporative cooling in organisms, it is not the main reason why water acts as a temperature stabilizer.

The correct option is high specific heat capacity. Specific heat capacity refers to the amount of heat energy required to raise the temperature of a substance by a specific amount. Water has a relatively high specific heat capacity compared to many other substances.Water's high specific heat capacity allows it to absorb and store a significant amount of heat energy without a substantial increase in temperature. As a result, water can act as a buffer against temperature fluctuations in its surroundings. During the daytime, when the environment is warmer, water absorbs heat from the sun and its surroundings. This absorption of heat prevents rapid temperature increases in the environment, helping to keep the surroundings relatively cooler. Conversely, during the nighttime, when the environment cools down, the water releases the stored heat energy, providing warmth and preventing rapid temperature drops. This property is particularly beneficial for many organisms that live in aquatic environments or have a high water content in their bodies, as it helps to maintain a relatively stable and hospitable temperature range for their biological processes.

Density refers to the mass of a substance per unit volume. Water has a unique property where its density decreases as it transitions from a liquid to a solid state (as in the case of ice). This property is why ice floats on water. While this property is ecologically important for aquatic ecosystems, it is not the primary reason why water acts as a temperature stabilizer.

This property is particularly beneficial for many organisms that live in aquatic environments or have a high water content in their bodies, as it helps to maintain a relatively stable and hospitable temperature range for their biological processes. Density: Density refers to the mass of a substance per unit volume. Water has a unique property where its density decreases as it transitions from a liquid to a solid state (as in the case of ice). This property is why ice floats on water. While this property is ecologically important for aquatic ecosystems, it is not the primary reason why water acts as a temperature stabilizer. In conclusion, option c) High specific heat capacity is the correct option. Water's high specific heat capacity allows it to act as a temperature stabilizer by absorbing and storing heat, buffering against rapid temperature changes in the environment and helping to maintain a relatively stable and suitable temperature range for organisms in their surroundings.

questions.text: Ice floats on water due to which property of water:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The correct option is low density. Water is one of the few substances where the solid form (ice) is less dense than the liquid form. When water cools down and freezes, its molecules arrange themselves in a hexagonal lattice structure, creating open spaces between the molecules. This unique arrangement leads to a decrease in the overall density of ice compared to liquid water. As a result, ice is lighter and less dense than the surrounding liquid water, causing it to float on the water's surface. This property of ice floating on water is crucial for aquatic ecosystems. When lakes and rivers freeze during colder temperatures, the layer of ice that forms on the surface acts as an insulating barrier, protecting the aquatic life beneath from extreme temperature fluctuations. If ice were denser than water, it would sink, leading to the freezing of entire water bodies from the bottom up, which would have severe consequences for the organisms living in these environments.

The heat of vaporization is the amount of heat energy required to convert a liquid into a gas at a constant temperature and pressure. While this property is important for processes like evaporative cooling, it is not directly related to why ice floats on water.

Ionization refers to the process by which a molecule or atom gains or loses electrons, becoming charged. Water can undergo ionization, where a small fraction of water molecules dissociate into positively charged hydrogen ions (H+) and negatively charged hydroxide ions (OH-). However, this process is not related to the floating of ice on water.
Hydrophobic exclusion is the tendency of nonpolar substances to aggregate and minimize their contact with water. This phenomenon is not directly related to the property of ice floating on water.

questions.text: Most abundant carbohydrate in nature is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Cellulose is the most abundant carbohydrate in nature. It is a complex polysaccharide made up of glucose molecules linked together in a linear chain. Cellulose is the primary structural component of plant cell walls, providing rigidity and strength to the cell. As one of the most abundant biopolymers on Earth, cellulose plays a crucial role in providing structural support to plants, contributing to their form and overall growth.

Polysaccharides are a class of carbohydrates composed of multiple sugar units (monosaccharides) linked together. Cellulose, starch, and glycogen are all examples of polysaccharides. While they are essential carbohydrates, the question specifically asks for the most abundant carbohydrate in nature, which is cellulose (option a).

Starch is another polysaccharide made up of glucose units but with a branched or helical structure. It serves as a major energy storage polysaccharide in plants. Plants store excess glucose in the form of starch, mainly in roots and seeds, to be used as a source of energy when needed.

Glycogen is a highly branched polysaccharide made up of glucose units. It is the primary energy storage polysaccharide in animals, found primarily in the liver and muscles. Glycogen serves as a readily available source of glucose, which can be broken down to provide energy during times of high energy demand, such as physical activity or fasting.

questions.text: All of the following yield glucose on complete hydrolysis except:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Starch is a polysaccharide composed of glucose units linked together in a branched or helical structure. It is the primary energy storage polysaccharide in plants. Complete hydrolysis of starch breaks down the glycosidic bonds between the glucose units, yielding individual glucose molecules.

Cellulose is another polysaccharide made up of glucose units, but it has a linear arrangement. It is the primary structural component of plant cell walls, providing rigidity and strength to the cell. Unlike starch, cellulose is not easily hydrolyzed by most organisms due to the specific arrangement of its glucose units. However, certain microorganisms and animals possess cellulase enzymes that can break down cellulose into glucose units through hydrolysis.

Glycogen is a highly branched polysaccharide composed of glucose units. It serves as the primary energy storage polysaccharide in animals, found primarily in the liver and muscles. Similar to starch, complete hydrolysis of glycogen breaks down the glycosidic bonds between the glucose units, yielding individual glucose molecules.

Chitin is a polysaccharide composed of N-acetylglucosamine units linked together. It is a structural component found in the exoskeletons of arthropods (e.g., insects, spiders, crustaceans) and the cell walls of fungi. Chitin is not composed of glucose units, so it does not yield glucose on complete hydrolysis. Instead, it breaks down into N-acetylglucosamine monomers.

questions.text: Glucose combines with _ to form milk sugar.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Glucose is a monosaccharide, which means it is a single sugar unit. While glucose is an important carbohydrate and serves as a source of energy for the body, it does not combine with itself to form milk sugar.

Galactose is another monosaccharide and is crucial for the formation of lactose, which is the milk sugar. Lactose is a disaccharide composed of one molecule of glucose and one molecule of galactose, linked together by a glycosidic bond. When lactose is hydrolyzed, it breaks down into its constituent monosaccharides, glucose, and galactose.
Fructose is a monosaccharide, and it is the sweetest of all naturally occurring sugars. While fructose is commonly found in fruits and honey, it does not combine with glucose to form milk sugar.
Mannose is another monosaccharide, and it is important for various cellular processes in the body. However, it does not combine with glucose to form milk sugar.

questions.text: Formation of a tri-saccharide involves release of _ water molecule.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option suggests that the formation of a tri-saccharide involves the release of only one water molecule. However, the correct answer requires considering the number of water molecules released during the formation of each glycosidic bond in a tri-saccharide.

This option suggests that the formation of a tri-saccharide involves the release of three water molecules. However, this is not accurate. The number of water molecules released during the formation of a glycosidic bond depends on the type of glycosidic linkage being formed.

The correct option is 2. The formation of a tri-saccharide involves the linking of three sugar units (monosaccharides) through two glycosidic bonds. When a glycosidic bond is formed, a molecule of water (H2O) is released as a byproduct. This is a condensation reaction, where the hydroxyl group (-OH) from one sugar and a hydrogen atom from another sugar combine to form water (H2O), while the remaining oxygen and carbon atoms form the glycosidic bond between the two sugars. Since two glycosidic bonds are formed in a tri-saccharide, two water molecules are released during the process.

This option suggests that the formation of a tri-saccharide involves the release of four water molecules. However, this is not accurate, as explained above. The formation of two glycosidic bonds in a tri-saccharide results in the release of two water molecules.

questions.text: Which of the following polysaccharide is present in human muscles abundantly?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Myoglobin is a protein found in muscle tissues that is responsible for storing and transporting oxygen within muscle cells. While myoglobin is abundant in muscle tissues, it is not a polysaccharide.
Actin and myosin are proteins that are major components of muscle fibers and are involved in muscle contraction. They are not polysaccharides.

Collagen is the most abundant protein in the human body and is a major component of connective tissues, such as tendons, ligaments, and skin. While it plays a crucial role in supporting and connecting tissues, collagen is not a polysaccharide.

The correct option is correct. Glycogen is a highly branched polysaccharide made up of glucose units and serves as the primary energy storage polysaccharide in animals, including humans. In human muscles, glycogen is stored in large amounts and acts as a readily available source of glucose during periods of increased energy demand, such as exercise. When energy is needed, glycogen is broken down into glucose units, which can be further utilized through cellular respiration to produce energy for muscle contraction and other cellular functions.

questions.text: Pick out the odd one:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Cellulose is a complex polysaccharide composed of glucose units linked together. It is the primary structural component of plant cell walls, providing rigidity and strength to plant cells. Cellulose is abundant in nature and an important structural carbohydrate.

Agar is a complex polysaccharide extracted from certain types of seaweed. It is composed of repeating units of agarobiose, which is a disaccharide formed from galactose and anhydrous galactose. Agar is commonly used in the laboratory as a culture medium for growing microorganisms and in the food industry as a thickener and stabilizer.

Galactose is a monosaccharide, a single sugar unit, and it is an essential component of many complex carbohydrates. While it is an important sugar in its own right, it stands out in this list as the only monosaccharide among the polysaccharides and disaccharides.

It is composed of galacturonic acid units linked together and is responsible for the gel-like consistency of some fruits when they are cooked or processed. Pectin is often used as a gelling agent in making jams and jellies.

questions.text: Which of the following carbons are present outside the ring of fructose?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option suggests that the 1st and 2nd carbon atoms are present outside the ring of fructose. However, this is not accurate.
This option suggests that only the 1st carbon atom is present outside the ring of fructose. However, this is not accurate either.
This option suggests that the 1st and 5th carbon atoms are present outside the ring of fructose. However, this is not accurate.

The correct option is 1st and 6th Carbon. Fructose is a monosaccharide with a five-membered ring structure called a furanose ring. The ring is formed by the intramolecular reaction between the ketone group (C=O) on the 2nd carbon and the hydroxyl group (-OH) on the 5th carbon. The 6th carbon remains outside the ring structure as part of the fructose molecule. Inside the five-membered furanose ring, there are four carbon atoms (2nd, 3rd, 4th, and 5th) and one oxygen atom, forming a closed ring structure. The 1st and 6th carbon atoms are located outside this ring and are part of the linear portion of the fructose molecule.

questions.text: ln a disaccharide, if carbon atoms are 12 then how many OH groups will be present?

The correct option is 8. In a disaccharide, there are two sugar units (monosaccharides) linked together by a glycosidic bond. Now, to become a disaccharide, a glycosidic bond needs to form. This requires two hydroxyl groups. A water molecule is released while C-O forms the glycosidic bond. Therefore, in the disaccharide, the total number of hydroxyl groups will be 10 - 2 = 8.

questions.text: Which of the following is non-reducing disaccharide sugar?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Mannose is a monosaccharide, not a disaccharide. It is a single sugar unit and does not fit the criteria for a non-reducing disaccharide.

The correct option is sucrose. Sucrose is a non-reducing disaccharide composed of one molecule of glucose and one molecule of fructose linked together by a glycosidic bond. It is commonly known as table sugar and is obtained from sugarcane or sugar beets. Sucrose is non-reducing because it lacks a free aldehyde or ketone group, which is required for a sugar to be considered reducing. Both glucose and fructose have their anomeric carbon involved in the glycosidic linkage, leaving no free aldehyde or ketone group to participate in a reducing reaction.

Maltose is a reducing disaccharide composed of two glucose molecules linked together by a glycosidic bond. It is formed during the breakdown of starch into smaller carbohydrate units. Maltose is reducing because one of its glucose units has a free aldehyde group on the anomeric carbon, which can participate in a reducing reaction.

Lactose is a reducing disaccharide composed of one molecule of glucose and one molecule of galactose linked together by a glycosidic bond. It is commonly known as milk sugar and is found in milk and dairy products. Lactose is reducing because the glucose unit in lactose has a free aldehyde group on the anomeric carbon, allowing it to participate in a reducing reaction.

questions.text: Which of the foIIowing sugar is mainly present in human blood?

questions.text: The simplest monosaccharide containing keto group is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Glyceraldehyde is a monosaccharide with three carbon atoms (a triose sugar). It contains an aldehyde functional group (a carbonyl group with a terminal -CHO). While it is a simple monosaccharide, it does not contain a keto group.

Glucose is a monosaccharide with six carbon atoms (a hexose sugar). It contains an aldehyde functional group in its linear form, known as an aldohexose. However, in its cyclic form, which is the most stable and prevalent in biological systems, it contains a hydroxyl group (-OH) on its first carbon atom (C1), making it an aldose and not a keto sugar.

The correct option is dihydroxyacetone. Dihydroxyacetone is the simplest monosaccharide containing a keto group. It is a three-carbon sugar with a ketone functional group (a carbonyl group within the carbon chain) on its central carbon (C2) and hydroxyl groups (-OH) on the other two carbon atoms.
Ribose is a monosaccharide with five carbon atoms (a pentose sugar). It contains an aldehyde functional group (an aldopentose) on its first carbon atom (C1), making it an aldose and not a keto sugar.

questions.text: Which of the following type of carbohydrates has high molecular weight and is sparingly soluble in water?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Monosaccharides are the simplest form of carbohydrates, and they cannot be hydrolyzed into smaller carbohydrates. They consist of a single sugar unit and have a relatively low molecular weight. Examples of monosaccharides include glucose, fructose, and galactose. Monosaccharides are usually highly soluble in water due to their small size and ability to form hydrogen bonds with water molecules.

Oligosaccharides are carbohydrates composed of a small number of monosaccharide units (typically 3 to 10 sugar units) linked together by glycosidic bonds. They have higher molecular weights than monosaccharides but are still relatively smaller than polysaccharides. Examples of oligosaccharides include raffinose and sucrose. Oligosaccharides can be soluble or sparingly soluble in water, depending on their size and structure.

Disaccharides are a type of oligosaccharide composed of two monosaccharide units linked together by a glycosidic bond. Examples of disaccharides include lactose, maltose, and sucrose. Disaccharides have higher molecular weights than monosaccharides but are still smaller than polysaccharides. They are usually soluble in water, with some exceptions like trehalose, which is less soluble.

The correct option is polysaccharides. Polysaccharides are large carbohydrates composed of multiple monosaccharide units linked together by glycosidic bonds. They have very high molecular weights, making them much larger and more complex than monosaccharides, oligosaccharides, or disaccharides. Examples of polysaccharides include starch, glycogen, and cellulose. Due to their large size and complex structures, many polysaccharides are sparingly soluble or insoluble in water.

questions.text: The bond that is formed between two monosaccharide units is called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: An ionic bond is a type of chemical bond that forms between two atoms when one atom donates an electron, becoming positively charged (cation), and the other atom accepts the electron, becoming negatively charged (anion). Ionic bonds occur between atoms with significantly different electronegativities, resulting in the transfer of electrons.

A hydrogen bond is a type of weak chemical bond that occurs between a hydrogen atom in a polar covalent bond and an electronegative atom (usually oxygen, nitrogen, or fluorine) in another molecule. Hydrogen bonding is important for the structure and properties of many molecules, but it is not the bond that forms between two monosaccharide units.

A peptide bond is a specific type of covalent bond that forms between the carboxyl group (COOH) of one amino acid and the amino group (NH2) of another amino acid during the formation of a protein. Peptide bonds are essential for building protein chains in a process called protein synthesis.

The correct option is glycosidic bond. A glycosidic bond is a type of covalent bond that forms between two monosaccharide units during the formation of disaccharides, oligosaccharides, and polysaccharides. It is formed when a hydroxyl group (-OH) of one monosaccharide reacts with the anomeric carbon (the carbon containing the carbonyl group) of another monosaccharide. This reaction involves the removal of a water molecule (H2O), resulting in the formation of a glycosidic linkage between the two monosaccharides.

questions.text: Proteins of hair, horns, feathers and other skin parts are:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Storage proteins are proteins that serve as a reservoir of amino acids in an organism. They are typically found in seeds, eggs, and other tissues where they store amino acids for future use. Proteins of hair, horns, feathers, and other skin parts do not primarily function as storage proteins because they are not used to store amino acids.

This option is correct. Proteins of hair, horns, feathers, and other skin parts are primarily structural proteins. Structural proteins provide support and shape to various body parts, such as hair, nails, horns, feathers, and the skin's outer layer. They have a fibrous and durable nature, which makes them well-suited for providing the necessary strength and integrity to these body parts.

Enzymatic proteins are proteins that act as biological catalysts, speeding up chemical reactions in living organisms. Enzymatic proteins play vital roles in metabolic pathways and other biochemical processes, but they are not the primary type of proteins found in hair, horns, feathers, and other skin parts.

Hormonal proteins are proteins that act as hormones, which are signaling molecules that regulate various physiological processes in the body. Hormonal proteins play essential roles in cellular communication and coordination, but they are not the main type of proteins found in hair, horns, feathers, and other skin parts.

questions.text: Identify the protein, which cannot be crystallized.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Antibodies can be crystallized. In fact, the crystallization of antibodies has been an essential technique in the field of X-ray crystallography to determine their three-dimensional structures. The structural information obtained from the crystallization of antibodies has been crucial for understanding their function and for developing therapeutic antibodies.

Haemoglobin can be crystallized. It was one of the first proteins to be successfully crystallized, and its crystal structure has provided valuable insights into the oxygen-binding mechanism and allosteric regulation of this important protein.

This option is correct. Fibrin is a fibrous protein and a major component of blood clots. While fibrin plays a crucial role in blood clotting, it is difficult to crystallize due to its complex and fibrous nature. The long, branching, and interconnected structure of fibrin makes it challenging to form well-ordered crystals suitable for X-ray crystallography or other structural studies.

Enzymes are proteins that act as biological catalysts and are essential for various biochemical processes. While some enzymes have been successfully crystallized, many enzymes are challenging to crystallize due to their dynamic and flexible nature, large size, and the presence of multiple active sites.

questions.text: Usually a polypeptide chain bends and folds upon itself to form a globular shape. This is protein's:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The term "primary conformation" is not a commonly used phrase in the context of protein structure. The correct term for the linear sequence of amino acids in a polypeptide chain is the "primary structure." It represents the order of amino acids in the protein, and it is the most basic level of protein structure.

This option is correct. The tertiary conformation refers to the three-dimensional arrangement of a single polypeptide chain. The polypeptide chain bends, folds, and twists upon itself to form a specific globular shape stabilized by various types of interactions, such as hydrogen bonding, disulfide bridges, hydrophobic interactions, and ionic interactions. The tertiary structure is essential for the protein's overall stability and function.

The secondary conformation refers to local regular structures that form within the polypeptide chain, such as alpha-helices and beta-sheets. These secondary structures are stabilized by hydrogen bonding between nearby amino acid residues. While secondary structures play a crucial role in the overall protein folding, they do not fully define the globular shape of the entire protein.

The quaternary conformation refers to the arrangement of multiple polypeptide chains (subunits) to form a functional protein complex. Not all proteins have a quaternary structure; it is only applicable to proteins composed of multiple subunits. Proteins with a quaternary structure have multiple polypeptide chains interacting to create a functional protein assembly.

The polypeptide chain bends, folds, and twists upon itself to form a specific globular shape stabilized by various types of interactions, such as hydrogen bonding, disulfide bridges, hydrophobic interactions, and ionic interactions. The tertiary structure is essential for the protein's overall stability and function.

questions.text: Which one of the following is used in synthesis of nucleic acid hormones, co-enzyme and amino acid for protein synthesis?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Carbon is the backbone of organic molecules, which are the building blocks of life. Organic compounds are primarily composed of carbon atoms bonded to hydrogen, oxygen, nitrogen, and other elements. The four most common types of organic molecules in biology are carbohydrates, lipids, proteins, and nucleic acids.DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid) and RNA (ribonucleic acid) are nucleic acids that store and transmit genetic information. They are made up of carbon, hydrogen, oxygen, nitrogen, and phosphorus. The sequence of carbon-based nucleotides in these molecules encodes the genetic instructions for an organism's development and function.

Hydrogen is a critical component of water (H2O), which is the universal solvent for all living organisms. Water is essential for life because it serves as a medium for chemical reactions, a transport medium for nutrients and waste, and a temperature regulator. Hydrogenase enzymes are present in various microorganisms, and they can catalyze the reversible conversion of molecular hydrogen (H2) to protons (H+) and electrons (e-). These enzymes are involved in hydrogen metabolism in certain bacteria.

Oxygen is essential for aerobic respiration, a metabolic process used by most living organisms, including animals and many microorganisms. During respiration, cells use oxygen to break down organic molecules (such as glucose) to produce energy in the form of adenosine triphosphate (ATP). This process involves the electron transport chain, where oxygen acts as the final electron acceptor to create a proton gradient and drive ATP synthesis.

Amino acids are the building blocks of proteins, and nitrogen is a critical component of amino acids. Each amino acid contains an amino group (NH2), which contributes nitrogen to the structure. The arrangement of amino acids in proteins determines their three-dimensional structure and, consequently, their functions in biological processes.Nitrogen is a fundamental element in nucleic acids, specifically DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid) and RNA (ribonucleic acid). The nitrogenous bases adenine (A), cytosine (C), guanine (G), thymine (T), and uracil (U) are key components of these molecules. The sequence of nitrogenous bases encodes genetic information and plays a central role in heredity and genetic expression.

questions.explanation: Carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen are also important elements in biological molecules, nitrogen stands out as the primary element associated with nucleic acids, co-enzymes, and amino acids, making it the correct answer in this context.

questions.text: Enzymes are molecules of one of the following group of biomolecules. Mark the correct answer which can also be defined as a biological catalyst:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is correct. Enzymes are a type of biomolecule known as proteins. Proteins are large molecules composed of amino acids linked together in a specific sequence. Enzymes are biological catalysts, which means they facilitate and speed up chemical reactions in living organisms without being consumed in the process. Enzymes play a crucial role in various metabolic processes, and they are involved in breaking down substrates, building new molecules, and regulating biochemical pathways.

Fats, also known as lipids, are another type of biomolecule, but they are not enzymes. Fats serve as a source of energy storage in the body and are involved in various functions, including providing insulation, cushioning organs, and serving as structural components of cell membranes. Unlike enzymes, fats do not act as catalysts in biochemical reactions.

Carbohydrates are a class of biomolecules that include sugars and starches. Carbohydrates are primarily used as a source of energy in living organisms. While some carbohydrates may play a role in enzymatic activity as enzyme co-factors (e.g. certain sugars can act as co-factors for some enzymes), carbohydrates themselves are not enzymes and do not function as biological catalysts.

Vitamins are essential organic compounds required in small amounts for various biochemical reactions in the body. They play important roles as co-factors for enzymes, helping enzymes perform their catalytic functions. While vitamins are important for enzymatic activity, vitamins themselves are not enzymes and do not function as biological catalysts.

questions.explanation: Enzymes are a type of biomolecule known as proteins. Proteins are large molecules composed of amino acids linked together in a specific sequence. Enzymes are biological catalysts, which means they facilitate and speed up chemical reactions in living organisms without being consumed in the process. Enzymes play a crucial role in various metabolic processes, and they are involved in breaking down substrates, building new molecules, and regulating biochemical pathways.

questions.text: Which molecular structure of enzyme is essential for activity of enzyme?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The primary structure of an enzyme refers to the linear sequence of amino acids in its polypeptide chain. While the primary structure is essential for determining the overall sequence of the enzyme's amino acids, it alone is not sufficient for the enzyme's activity. The primary structure provides the basic information for the folding and formation of higher-order structures.

The secondary structure of an enzyme refers to the local folding patterns of the polypeptide chain, such as alpha-helices and beta-sheets, stabilized by hydrogen bonding. While the secondary structure contributes to the enzyme's stability, it is not the key determinant of its catalytic activity.

The quaternary structure of an enzyme refers to the arrangement of multiple polypeptide chains (subunits) in a multisubunit enzyme. Not all enzymes have a quaternary structure, and for many enzymes, the quaternary structure is not essential for their activity. Only enzymes composed of multiple subunits possess a quaternary structure.

This option is correct. The tertiary structure of an enzyme refers to the three-dimensional arrangement of its polypeptide chain, including the interactions between different regions of the chain. The tertiary structure is crucial for the enzyme's catalytic activity because it determines the specific folding and positioning of the enzyme's active site. The active site is a region of the enzyme where the substrate binds and the chemical reaction takes place. The unique three-dimensional arrangement of amino acid residues in the active site allows the enzyme to interact specifically with its substrate and catalyze the reaction with high efficiency.

questions.explanation: The tertiary structure of an enzyme refers to the three-dimensional arrangement of its polypeptide chain, including the interactions between different regions of the chain. The tertiary structure is crucial for the enzyme's catalytic activity because it determines the specific folding and positioning of the enzyme's active site. The active site is a region of the enzyme where the substrate binds and the chemical reaction takes place. The unique three-dimensional arrangement of amino acid residues in the active site allows the enzyme to interact specifically with its substrate and catalyze the reaction with high efficiency.

questions.text: Secondary structure of protein is found in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Trypsin is an enzyme, and enzymes are generally not known for having well-defined secondary structures like alpha-helices or beta-sheets. While enzymes may have some local secondary structures in certain regions, their overall structure is mainly influenced by the tertiary and quaternary structures, which are necessary for their catalytic activity. Therefore, trypsin is not known for having a significant secondary structure.

Insulin is a peptide hormone composed of two polypeptide chains linked together by disulfide bonds. Peptide hormones like insulin may have some elements of secondary structure, but their overall structure is influenced by the interactions between the chains and the presence of disulfide bonds, which are part of the tertiary and quaternary structures. Thus, insulin does not exhibit significant secondary structure.

This option is correct. Keratin is a fibrous protein found in structures like hair, nails, feathers, and the outer layer of the skin. Keratin is well-known for its secondary structure, which is primarily composed of alpha-helices. The repetitive sequences of amino acids in keratin promote the formation of alpha-helical regions, giving it strength and stability. This secondary structure allows keratin to form durable and resistant filaments that provide mechanical support to various biological structures.

Glucagon is a peptide hormone that plays a role in regulating blood glucose levels. Like insulin, glucagon is composed of a specific sequence of amino acids that may form some local secondary structures. However, its overall structure is influenced by the interactions between the polypeptide chains and any disulfide bonds present, which are characteristics of tertiary and quaternary structures. Glucagon is not primarily known for its extensive secondary structure.

Keratin is well-known for its secondary structure, which is primarily composed of alpha-helices. The repetitive sequences of amino acids in keratin promote the formation of alpha-helical regions, giving it strength and stability. This secondary structure allows keratin to form durable and resistant filaments that provide mechanical support to various biological structures.

options.explanation: This option is correct. Most proteins are made up of 20 types of standard amino acids. These 20 amino acids are commonly referred to as proteinogenic amino acids and serve as the building blocks for constructing the vast majority of proteins in living organisms. Each of these 20 amino acids has a unique side chain (R-group) that imparts specific chemical properties to the amino acid, allowing for the wide diversity of protein structures and functions.

This option is incorrect. While it is true that about 25 amino acids are constituents of proteins, the majority of proteins are still made up of only 20 types of standard amino acids. These 25 amino acids include the 20 standard amino acids found in most proteins, along with some non-standard or modified amino acids that occur in specific proteins or under certain conditions.

This option is incorrect. While about 170 different types of amino acids have been found to occur in cells and tissues, most proteins are not made up of such a large number of different amino acids. As mentioned earlier, only 20 types of standard amino acids are commonly used to build proteins, making up the bulk of the protein sequences in living organisms.

This option is incorrect. There are not 200 amino acids that make up most proteins. As explained earlier, the vast majority of proteins are constructed from 20 types of standard amino acids. The number 200 is significantly higher than the actual number of amino acids commonly used in protein synthesis.

questions.explanation: About 170 types of amino acids have been found to occur in cells and tissues. Of these, about 25 are constituents of proteins. Most of the proteins are, however, made of 20 types of amino acids.

questions.text: Backbone of amino acid comprises of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is correct. The backbone of an amino acid is composed of a central carbon (C) atom, a hydrogen (H) atom, an amino (NH2) group, and a carboxyl (COOH) group. The central carbon atom is bonded to the amino group, the carboxyl group, and a hydrogen atom, forming a linear chain of C-C-N.

While the amino (NH2) group is indeed a part of the amino acid structure, it is only one component of the amino acid's backbone. The backbone, as mentioned in option A, also includes the central carbon atom and the carboxyl group, making it a C-C-N backbone.

Similar to the explanation for option B, the carboxyl (COOH) group is indeed part of the amino acid structure, but it is not the entire backbone. The backbone comprises the central carbon atom, the amino group, and the carboxyl group, resulting in a C-C-N backbone.

The hydrogen (H) atom is indeed one component of the amino acid structure, but it is not the backbone of the amino acid. The backbone is formed by the central carbon atom, the amino group, and the carboxyl group, as explained in option A.

questions.explanation: The backbone of an amino acid is composed of a central carbon (C) atom, a hydrogen (H) atom, an amino (NH2) group, and a carboxyl (COOH) group. questions.text: Bonds present in alpha helix are:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is correct. The bonds present in an alpha-helix are hydrogen bonds. In an alpha-helix, the backbone of the polypeptide chain forms a helical structure stabilized by hydrogen bonds between the carbonyl oxygen atom of one amino acid and the amide hydrogen atom of another amino acid. These hydrogen bonds occur between the carbonyl group (C=O) of the nth amino acid and the amide group (N-H) of the (n+4)th amino acid along the chain, creating a stable and regular helical conformation.

Ionic bonds are formed between charged amino acid side chains (R-groups) that contain positively charged (cationic) and negatively charged (anionic) groups. While ionic interactions can play a role in protein structure and stability, they are not the primary bonds that stabilize an alpha-helix.

Disulfide linkages are covalent bonds formed between two cysteine amino acids through the oxidation of their sulfhydryl (-SH) groups. These bonds create disulfide bridges that can contribute to protein folding and stability, but they are not the bonds present in an alpha-helix.

Peptide bonds are covalent bonds formed between the carboxyl (COOH) group of one amino acid and the amino (NH2) group of another amino acid during protein synthesis. Peptide bonds link amino acids together to form the polypeptide chain, but they are not the specific bonds that stabilize an alpha helix.

questions.explanation: The bonds present in an alpha helix are hydrogen bonds. In an alpha helix, the backbone of the polypeptide chain forms a helical structure stabilized by hydrogen bonds between the carbonyl oxygen atom of one amino acid and the amide hydrogen atom of another amino acid. These hydrogen bonds occur between the carbonyl group (C=O) of the nth amino acid and the amide group (N-H) of the (n+4)th amino acid along the chain, creating a stable and regular helical conformation.

questions.text: Sequence of amino acids is important in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is correct. The sequence of amino acids in a polypeptide chain, known as the primary structure, is crucially important for determining a protein's overall function and properties. The sequence dictates how the amino acids are arranged, and this specific order influences how the protein folds into its unique three-dimensional structure. The primary structure is the foundation upon which the higher-order structures of the protein, such as the secondary, tertiary, and quaternary structures, are built.

While the tertiary structure of a protein is indeed important for its function, it is not primarily determined by the sequence of amino acids. The tertiary structure refers to the three-dimensional arrangement of the entire polypeptide chain, including how different regions of the protein fold and interact. While the primary structure sets the sequence of amino acids, it is the interactions between the side chains (R-groups) of these amino acids that largely determine the protein's final three-dimensional shape in the tertiary structure.

The secondary structure of a protein is determined by local folding patterns of the polypeptide chain, specifically the formation of alpha-helices and beta-sheets. The primary structure sets the sequence of amino acids that will fold into these secondary structures, but the primary structure alone does not dictate the specific secondary structure elements. The interactions between nearby amino acids, specifically the hydrogen bonding patterns between the carbonyl oxygen and amide hydrogen, drive the formation of secondary structures.

The quaternary structure refers to the arrangement of multiple polypeptide chains (subunits) in a multisubunit protein. The primary structure is crucial for each individual subunit, but it does not determine the specific arrangement of multiple subunits in the quaternary structure. The quaternary structure is formed by interactions between the subunits, which may involve hydrogen bonding, hydrophobic interactions, ionic interactions, and disulfide bridges, depending on the specific protein.

questions.explanation: The sequence of amino acids in a polypeptide chain, known as the primary structure, is crucially important for determining a protein's overall function and properties. The sequence dictates how the amino acids are arranged, and this specific order influences how the protein folds into its unique three-dimensional structure. The primary structure is the foundation upon which the higher-order structures of the protein, such as the secondary, tertiary, and quaternary structures, are built.

questions.text: The structure of a fibrous protein comprises of polypeptide chains in the form of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. A cluster refers to a group of items that are closely packed together. It does not describe the structure of a fibrous protein, which is characterized by its elongated and thread-like nature.

This option is incorrect. A spherical or curled up ball describes a globular protein's structure, not a fibrous protein. Globular proteins have a compact, three-dimensional shape, while fibrous proteins have a more elongated and linear structure.

This option is incorrect. A flat uncoiled chain does not describe the structure of a fibrous protein. Fibrous proteins have tightly packed, repetitive sequences of amino acids, giving rise to their elongated and fibrous nature, not flat and uncoiled chains.

This option is correct. Fibrous proteins are characterized by their long, elongated strands or fibrils. They have a repeating pattern of secondary structures, such as alpha helices or beta sheets, that align along the length of the protein. This arrangement imparts tensile strength and structural integrity, making fibrous proteins well-suited for providing support and structure in various tissues and cells.

questions.explanation: Fibrous proteins are characterized by their long, elongated strands or fibrils. They have a repeating pattern of secondary structures, such as alpha helices or beta sheets, that align along the length of the protein. questions.text: Which of the following is a protease?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Deaminase is an enzyme that catalyzes the removal of an amino group (NH2) from an amino acid. It is involved in processes such as the breakdown of amino acids and the conversion of one amino acid to another. Deaminases are not proteases.

Protease is an enzyme that cleaves peptide bonds. Rennin, also known as chymosin, is a protease enzyme that plays a crucial role in the digestion of milk proteins in the stomach of young mammals, especially in the early stages of life. Rennin helps coagulate milk, forming a curd that aids in the digestion and absorption of nutrients.
Oxidase is a class of enzymes that catalyze the transfer of electrons from a substrate to an oxygen molecule. They are involved in various redox reactions in the cell. Oxidases are not proteases.

Catalase is an enzyme that catalyzes the decomposition of hydrogen peroxide into water and oxygen. It is involved in detoxifying hydrogen peroxide, a harmful byproduct of cellular metabolism. Catalase is not a protease.

questions.text: It is an amino acid and also a part of phospholipid:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Serine is an amino acid and is also a part of phospholipids. It is one of the 20 standard amino acids used in protein synthesis, and it serves as a head group in certain phospholipids, such as phosphatidylserine, which is a key component of cell membranes.
Ethanolamine is an amino alcohol and is also a part of phospholipids. It is found as a head group in phosphatidylethanolamine, another important phospholipid in biological membranes.

Choline is a part of phospholipids, specifically phosphatidylcholine, but it is not an amino acid. It is a quaternary ammonium compound and contributes to the structure and function of cell membranes.
Aspartic acid is an amino acid, but it is not a part of phospholipids. It is one of the 20 standard amino acids used in protein synthesis.

questions.text: Myelin sheath of neuron is composed of:

It forms a part of the lipid-rich layer that makes up the myelin sheath.

questions.text: Which of following can form a biopolymer?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Yes, amino acids are the building blocks of biopolymers. Biopolymers are large molecules made up of repeating units of smaller molecules. Amino acids are the repeating units of proteins, which are the most abundant biopolymers in the human body. Other biopolymers that are made up of amino acids include peptides, polypeptides, and polyamides.

Yes, nucleotides form biopolymers. A biopolymer is a large molecule composed of repeating units of smaller molecules called monomers. In the case of nucleotides, the monomers are linked together by phosphodiester bonds to form nucleic acids, which are the genetic material of all living organisms.

Yes, monosaccharides can form biopolymers. Biopolymers are large molecules made up of repeating units of smaller molecules, called monomers. Monosaccharides are the monomers that make up biopolymers called polysaccharides. Polysaccharides are very important molecules in living organisms. They are used for energy storage, structural support, and cell signaling.

questions.text: Lipids contain double amount of energy as compared to the same amount of carbohydrates due to the presence of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is incorrect. Lipids contain more C-H bonds than carbohydrates, which is why they contain more energy. Carbohydrates are made up of carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen atoms. The ratio of hydrogen to oxygen atoms in carbohydrates is 2:1. This means that for every two hydrogen atoms in a carbohydrate, there is one oxygen atom. Lipids are also made up of carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen atoms. However, the ratio of hydrogen to oxygen atoms in lipids is 3:1. This means that for every three hydrogen atoms in a lipid, there is one oxygen atom.

Yes, that is correct. Lipids contain more energy than carbohydrates because they have a higher proportion of carbon-hydrogen bonds. Carbon-hydrogen bonds are very strong and require a lot of energy to break. When lipids are broken down, the energy from these bonds is released. Oxygen-hydrogen bonds are not as strong as carbon-hydrogen bonds, so they release less energy when they are broken down. This is why lipids are considered to be a more concentrated source of energy than carbohydrates. A gram of fat contains about 9 calories, while a gram of carbohydrate contains about 4 calories.

questions.text: Lipids are organic molecules which are insoluble in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The solubility of lipids in alcohol depends on the structure of the lipid. Triglycerides have a very hydrophobic (water-fearing) structure, which makes them insoluble in alcohol. Phospholipids, on the other hand, have a more hydrophilic (water-loving) structure, which makes them soluble in alcohol. In general, lipids with shorter hydrocarbon chains are more soluble in alcohol than lipids with longer hydrocarbon chains. This is because the shorter hydrocarbon chains have more exposed hydrogen atoms, which can interact with the water molecules in the alcohol.

Lipids are organic molecules that are soluble in ether. Ether is a non-polar solvent, and lipids are also non-polar molecules. This means that the lipids can interact with the ether molecules through van der Waals forces.

Lipids are organic molecules that are soluble in chloroform. Chloroform is a non-polar solvent, and lipids are also non-polar molecules. This means that the hydrophobic parts of the lipid molecules can interact with the hydrophobic parts of the chloroform molecules, allowing the lipids to dissolve in the chloroform.

Lipids are organic molecules that are insoluble in water. This is because lipids are non-polar molecules, while water is a polar molecule. Non-polar molecules do not dissolve in polar solvents, such as water.

questions.text: What type of linkage is formed when an alcohol and an acid react?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: No, an ionic bond is not formed when an alcohol and an acid react. The reaction between an alcohol and an acid is called esterification, and it forms an ester. An ester is a covalent compound, which means that it is held together by covalent bonds.

A glycosidic linkage is formed when the hydroxyl group of a sugar molecule reacts with the carbonyl group of another sugar molecule, releasing a molecule of water. This reaction is called a condensation reaction. The reaction between an alcohol and an acid is called an esterification reaction.

When an alcohol and an acid react, they form an ester linkage. An ester linkage is a type of covalent bond that is formed between an alcohol group and a carboxylic acid group. The general formula for an ester is RCOOR', where R and R' are alkyl groups. The reaction between an alcohol and an acid is called an esterification reaction. The esterification reaction is a condensation reaction, which means that it produces a water molecule as a byproduct. The esterification reaction is catalyzed by an acid, such as sulfuric acid. The esterification reaction is reversible, which means that the ester can be broken down back into the alcohol and the acid. However, the esterification reaction is usually carried out under conditions that favor the formation of the ester.

questions.text: The head of phospholipid molecule is polar (hydrophilic) due to:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The head of a phospholipid molecule is polar (hydrophilic) due to the phosphate group, not the fatty acids. The phosphate group is negatively charged, which makes it polar and attracted to water. The fatty acids, on the other hand, are non-polar and hydrophobic. This is why the phospholipid molecule forms a bilayer, with the polar heads facing outwards towards the water and the non-polar tails facing inwards away from the water.

The head of a phospholipid molecule is polar (hydrophilic) due to the presence of a phosphate group. The phosphate group is negatively charged, which makes it attracted to water molecules. The water molecules surround the phosphate group, which helps to keep the phospholipid molecule stable in an aqueous environment. In addition to the phosphate group, the head of a phospholipid molecule may also contain other polar groups, such as a choline group or an ethanolamine group. These groups are also attracted to water molecules, which further contributes to the hydrophilicity of the head of the phospholipid molecule.

The carboxylic acids are also polar, but they are not as polar as the phosphate group. The carboxylic acids are also hydrophobic, which means that they are repelled by water. This is why the fatty acid tails of a phospholipid molecule are hydrophobic and point away from water.
